1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# setup.sh - cw setup command
     3→
     4→# Arguments: [prefill_name]
     5→#   prefill_name ... passed when called from cw new to skip name input
     6→cmd_setup() {
     7→  require_jq
     8→
     9→  local prefill_name="${1:-}"
    10→
    11→  local target_dir
    12→  target_dir="$(pwd)"
    13→
    14→  # Check if already set up as workspace
    15→  if is_workspace "$target_dir"; then
    16→    warn "$(t "workspace_already_setup")"
    17→    local existing_name
    18→    existing_name=$(ws_get "$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE" '.name')
    19→    echo "  $(t "workspace_name"): $(bold "$existing_name")"
    20→    echo ""
    21→    read -rep "  $(t "re_setup") [y/N]: " confirm
    22→    [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]] || { info "$(t "cancel")"; exit 0; }
    23→    echo ""
    24→  fi
    25→
    26→  echo ""
    27→  echo "$(bold "Claude Workspace Setup")"
    28→  echo "$(dim "$(t "directory"): $target_dir")"
    29→  echo ""
    30→
    31→  # ──────────────────────────────
    32→  # Step 1: Workspace name
    33→  # ──────────────────────────────
    34→  step "Step 1: $(t "setup_step1")"
    35→  local ws_name
    36→  if [[ -n "$prefill_name" ]]; then
    37→    # Called from cw new, name already set
    38→    ws_name="$prefill_name"
    39→    success "$(t "workspace_name"): $ws_name"
    40→  else
    41→    local default_name
    42→    default_name="$(basename "$target_dir")"
    43→    read -rep "  $(t "workspace_name") [${default_name}]: " ws_name
    44→    ws_name="${ws_name:-$default_name}"
    45→    success "$(t "workspace_name"): $ws_name"
    46→  fi
    47→
    48→  # ──────────────────────────────
    49→  # Step 2: Description
    50→  # ──────────────────────────────
    51→  step "Step 2: $(t "setup_step2")"
    52→  read -rep "  $(t "workspace_desc"): " ws_description
    53→  ws_description="${ws_description:-}"
    54→
    55→  # ──────────────────────────────
    56→  # Step 3: Add target directories
    57→  # ──────────────────────────────
    58→  step "Step 3: $(t "setup_step3")"
    59→  echo "  $(dim "$(t "setup_path_hint")")"
    60→  echo "  $(dim "Example: ~/repos/my-project")"
    61→  echo ""
    62→
    63→  local dirs=()
    64→  local dir_roles=()
    65→
    66→  while true; do
    67→    local raw_path
    68→    raw_path=$(select_dir_with_fzf "$(t "select_dir_prompt")")
    69→    [[ -z "$raw_path" ]] && break
    70→
    71→    local expanded_path="$raw_path"
    72→
    73→    if [[ ! -d "$expanded_path" ]]; then
    74→      warn "$(t "dir_not_found"): $expanded_path"
    75→      read -rep "  $(t "add_anyway") [y/N]: " force
    76→      [[ "$force" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]] || continue
    77→    fi
    78→
    79→    # Role label (optional)
    80→    read -rep "  $(t "dir_add_role"): " role
    81→    role="${role:-}"
    82→
    83→    dirs+=("$expanded_path")
    84→    dir_roles+=("$role")
    85→    success "$(t "dir_added"): $expanded_path $([ -n "$role" ] && echo "(${role})")"
    86→  done
    87→
    88→  if [[ ${#dirs[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    89→    warn "No directories specified. Use cw add-dir later to add."
    90→  fi
    91→
    92→  # ──────────────────────────────
    93→  # Step 4: Generate workspace.json
    94→  # ──────────────────────────────
    95→  step "Step 4: $(t "setup_step4")"
    96→
    97→  local ws_file="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE"
    98→  local created_at
    99→  created_at=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
   100→
   101→  # Build JSON
   102→  local dirs_json="[]"
   103→  for i in "${!dirs[@]}"; do
   104→    local dir="${dirs[$i]}"
   105→    local role="${dir_roles[$i]}"
   106→    dirs_json=$(echo "$dirs_json" | jq \
   107→      --arg path "$dir" \
   108→      --arg role "$role" \
   109→      '. += [{"path": $path, "role": $role}]')
   110→  done
   111→
   112→  jq -n \
   113→    --arg name "$ws_name" \
   114→    --arg description "$ws_description" \
   115→    --arg created_at "$created_at" \
   116→    --arg workspace_path "$target_dir" \
   117→    --argjson dirs "$dirs_json" \
   118→    '{
   119→      name: $name,
   120→      description: $description,
   121→      workspace_path: $workspace_path,
   122→      created_at: $created_at,
   123→      dirs: $dirs
   124→    }' > "$ws_file"
   125→
   126→  success "$(t "created"): $ws_file"
   127→
   128→  # ──────────────────────────────
   129→  # Step 5: Generate CLAUDE.md
   130→  # ──────────────────────────────
   131→  step "Step 5: $(t "setup_step5")"
   132→
   133→  local claude_md="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_CLAUDE_MD"
   134→  _generate_workspace_claude_md "$claude_md" "$ws_name" "$ws_description" "$ws_file"
   135→  success "$(t "created"): $claude_md"
   136→
   137→  # ──────────────────────────────
   138→  # Step 6: Install skills to .claude/skills/
   139→  # ──────────────────────────────
   140→  step "Step 6: $(t "setup_step6")"
   141→
   142→  if [[ -d "$CW_SKILLS_DIR" ]]; then
   143→    local ws_skills_dir="$target_dir/.claude/skills"
   144→    mkdir -p "$ws_skills_dir"
   145→    for skill_dir in "$CW_SKILLS_DIR"/*/; do
   146→      [[ -d "$skill_dir" ]] || continue
   147→      local skill_name
   148→      skill_name="$(basename "$skill_dir")"
   149→      cp -R "$skill_dir" "$ws_skills_dir/$skill_name"
   150→    done
   151→    success "$(t "setup_skills_installed"): .claude/skills/"
   152→  fi
   153→
   154→  # ──────────────────────────────
   155→  # Step 7: Register to global registry
   156→  # ──────────────────────────────
   157→  step "Step 7: $(t "setup_step7")"
   158→  registry_add "$ws_name" "$target_dir"
   159→  success "$(t "registered"): ~/.claude-workspace/registry.json"
   160→
   161→  # ──────────────────────────────
   162→  # Complete
   163→  # ──────────────────────────────
   164→  echo ""
   165→  echo "$(green "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
   166→  echo "$(green "  $(t "setup_complete")")"
   167→  echo "$(green "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
   168→  echo ""
   169→  echo "  $(t "setup_run")"
   170→  echo "  $(bold "  cw")"
   171→  echo ""
   172→}
   173→
   174→# Generate workspace CLAUDE.md template
   175→# Arguments: <claude_md_path> <ws_name> <ws_description> <ws_file_path>
   176→_generate_workspace_claude_md() {
   177→  local claude_md="$1"
   178→  local ws_name="$2"
   179→  local ws_description="$3"
   180→  local ws_file="$4"
   181→
   182→  # Build Linked Repositories table rows
   183→  local dirs_table=""
   184→  if [[ -f "$ws_file" ]]; then
   185→    local count
   186→    count=$(jq '.dirs | length' "$ws_file")
   187→    for ((i=0; i<count; i++)); do
   188→      local path role
   189→      path=$(jq -r ".dirs[$i].path" "$ws_file")
   190→      role=$(jq -r ".dirs[$i].role // \"\"" "$ws_file")
   191→      local label="${role:-$(basename "$path")}"
   192→      dirs_table="${dirs_table}| ${label} | \`${path}\` |"$'\n'
   193→    done
   194→  fi
   195→
   196→  local created_at
   197→  created_at=$(date "+%Y-%m-%d")
   198→
   199→  # ${ws_name,,} requires bash 4+, use tr for macOS bash 3.2 compatibility
   200→  local ws_name_lower
   201→  ws_name_lower=$(echo "$ws_name" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr ' ' '-')
   202→
   203→  # Use printf to avoid heredoc variable expansion issues
   204→  printf '# Workspace: %s\n\n> %s\n\nCreated: %s\n\n---\n\n## Linked Repositories\n\n| Role | Path |\n|------|------|\n%s\n## Workflow Rules\n\n- **REQUIRED**: Use the `workspace-task-manager` skill for task decomposition and management\n  - Decompose workspace goals into tasks before starting implementation\n  - Each task gets a `YYYY-MM-DD-{task-name}/` directory under this workspace\n  - Follow the superpowers workflow (brainstorming → writing-plans → execute) per task\n  - Save design docs and plans into the task directory, not `docs/plans/`\n- Branch naming convention: `feature/ws-%s-*`\n\n## Current Tasks\n\n| Status | Task | Directory | Priority |\n|--------|------|-----------|----------|\n| | (Use workspace-task-manager skill to populate) | | |\n\n## Notes\n\nRecord notes and design decisions here.\n' \
   205→    "$ws_name" \
   206→    "${ws_description:-Describe this workspace here.}" \
   207→    "$created_at" \
   208→    "$dirs_table" \
   209→    "$ws_name_lower" \
   210→    > "$claude_md"
   211→}
   212→
   213→# cw add-dir command
   214→cmd_add_dir() {
   215→  require_jq
   216→
   217→  local target_dir
   218→  target_dir="$(pwd)"
   219→
   220→  if ! is_workspace "$target_dir"; then
   221→    error "$(t "workspace_not_found"). Run cw setup first."
   222→    exit 1
   223→  fi
   224→
   225→  local ws_file="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE"
   226→  local ws_name
   227→  ws_name=$(ws_get "$ws_file" '.name')
   228→
   229→  echo ""
   230→  echo "$(bold "$(t "add_directory"): $ws_name")"
   231→  echo ""
   232→
   233→  local new_path="${1:-}"
   234→  if [[ -z "$new_path" ]]; then
   235→    new_path=$(select_dir_with_fzf "$(t "select_dir_prompt")")
   236→  fi
   237→  [[ -z "$new_path" ]] && { info "$(t "cancel")"; exit 0; }
   238→
   239→  local expanded_path
   240→  expanded_path=$(expand_path "$new_path")
   241→
   242→  if [[ ! -d "$expanded_path" ]]; then
   243→    warn "$(t "dir_not_found"): $expanded_path"
   244→    read -rep "  $(t "add_anyway") [y/N]: " force
   245→    [[ "$force" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]] || exit 0
   246→  fi
   247→
   248→  # Check if already registered
   249→  if jq -e --arg p "$expanded_path" '.dirs[] | select(.path == $p)' "$ws_file" &>/dev/null; then
   250→    warn "$(t "already_exists"): $expanded_path"
   251→    exit 0
   252→  fi
   253→
   254→  read -rep "  $(t "dir_add_role"): " role
   255→
   256→  # Add to workspace.json
   257→  ws_set "$ws_file" \
   258→    --arg path "$expanded_path" \
   259→    --arg role "${role:-}" \
   260→    '.dirs += [{"path": $path, "role": $role}]'
   261→
   262→  # Update CLAUDE.md (regenerate Linked Repositories table)
   263→  local claude_md="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_CLAUDE_MD"
   264→  local ws_description
   265→  ws_description=$(ws_get "$ws_file" '.description')
   266→  _generate_workspace_claude_md "$claude_md" "$ws_name" "$ws_description" "$ws_file"
   267→
   268→  success "$(t "dir_added"): $expanded_path"
   269→}
   270→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# menu.sh - Interactive Workspace selection menu
     3→
     4→# Module-level arrays (no nameref needed, bash 3.2 compatible)
     5→_CW_MENU_PATHS=()
     6→_CW_MENU_NAMES=()
     7→_CW_MENU_TIMES=()
     8→_CW_MENU_REGISTERED=()
     9→
    10→# ──────────────────────────────
    11→# Build workspace list into module arrays
    12→# ──────────────────────────────
    13→_cw_menu_build_list() {
    14→  _CW_MENU_PATHS=()
    15→  _CW_MENU_NAMES=()
    16→  _CW_MENU_TIMES=()
    17→  _CW_MENU_REGISTERED=()
    18→
    19→  local ws_list
    20→  ws_list=$(registry_list)
    21→
    22→  while IFS= read -r ws; do
    23→    local name path last_used
    24→    name=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.name')
    25→    path=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.path')
    26→    last_used=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.last_used // ""')
    27→    _CW_MENU_PATHS+=("$path")
    28→    _CW_MENU_NAMES+=("$name")
    29→    _CW_MENU_TIMES+=("$last_used")
    30→    _CW_MENU_REGISTERED+=("true")
    31→  done < <(echo "$ws_list" | jq -c '.[]' 2>/dev/null)
    32→
    33→  local search_base="${WORKING_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/WorkingProjects}"
    34→  if [[ -d "$search_base" ]]; then
    35→    while IFS= read -r ws_file; do
    36→      local ws_dir ws_name
    37→      ws_dir=$(dirname "$ws_file")
    38→      ws_name=$(ws_get "$ws_file" '.name')
    39→      local already=false
    40→      local p
    41→      for p in "${_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]+"${_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"}"; do
    42→        [[ "$p" == "$ws_dir" ]] && already=true && break
    43→      done
    44→      $already && continue
    45→      _CW_MENU_PATHS+=("$ws_dir")
    46→      _CW_MENU_NAMES+=("$ws_name")
    47→      _CW_MENU_TIMES+=("")
    48→      _CW_MENU_REGISTERED+=("false")
    49→    done < <(find "$search_base" -maxdepth 2 -name ".workspace.json" 2>/dev/null | sort)
    50→  fi
    51→}
    52→
    53→# ──────────────────────────────
    54→# Main entry point
    55→# ──────────────────────────────
    56→cmd_menu() {
    57→  require_jq
    58→  _cw_menu_build_list
    59→
    60→  while true; do
    61→    local selected_path
    62→    if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
    63→      selected_path=$(_menu_fzf_pick)
    64→    else
    65→      selected_path=$(_menu_numbered_pick)
    66→    fi
    67→
    68→    # Empty = Esc/quit
    69→    if [[ -z "$selected_path" ]]; then
    70→      echo ""
    71→      info "$(t "cmd_quit")"
    72→      return 0
    73→    fi
    74→
    75→    # Create new workspace
    76→    if [[ "$selected_path" == "CREATE_NEW" ]]; then
    77→      cmd_new
    78→      return
    79→    fi
    80→
    81→    # Cleanup registry
    82→    if [[ "$selected_path" == "CLEANUP" ]]; then
    83→      _menu_cleanup
    84→      _cw_menu_build_list
    85→      continue
    86→    fi
    87→
    88→    # Auto-register if unregistered
    89→    local idx
    90→    for idx in "${!_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"; do
    91→      if [[ "${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$idx]}" == "$selected_path" ]]; then
    92→        if [[ "${_CW_MENU_REGISTERED[$idx]}" == "false" ]]; then
    93→          registry_add "${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}" "$selected_path"
    94→          success "$(t "registered"): ${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}"
    95→        fi
    96→        break
    97→      fi
    98→    done
    99→
   100→    if [[ ! -d "$selected_path" ]]; then
   101→      error "$(t "dir_not_found"): $selected_path"
   102→      continue
   103→    fi
   104→
   105→    local ws_name
   106→    ws_name=$(jq -r '.name' "${selected_path}/${WORKSPACE_FILE}" 2>/dev/null)
   107→
   108→    # Show submenu and handle result
   109→    local submenu_result
   110→    submenu_result=$(_menu_submenu_pick "$selected_path" "$ws_name")
   111→
   112→    case "$submenu_result" in
   113→      LAUNCH)
   114→        cd "$selected_path"
   115→        cmd_launch
   116→        return
   117→        ;;
   118→      ADD_DIR)
   119→        (cd "$selected_path" && cmd_add_dir) || true
   120→        _cw_menu_build_list
   121→        # Re-show submenu after add-dir
   122→        submenu_result=$(_menu_submenu_pick "$selected_path" "$ws_name")
   123→        [[ "$submenu_result" == "LAUNCH" ]] && { cd "$selected_path"; cmd_launch; return; }
   124→        continue
   125→        ;;
   126→      INFO)
   127→        (cd "$selected_path" && cmd_info) || true
   128→        echo ""
   129→        read -rp "  $(t "press_enter"): " _
   130→        continue
   131→        ;;
   132→      FINDER)
   133→        open "$selected_path"
   134→        continue
   135→        ;;
   136→      FORGET)
   137→        _menu_forget "$selected_path" "$ws_name"
   138→        _cw_menu_build_list
   139→        continue
   140→        ;;
   141→      *)
   142→        # BACK or empty — loop back to main list
   143→        continue
   144→        ;;
   145→    esac
   146→  done
   147→}
   148→
   149→# ──────────────────────────────
   150→# fzf picker: returns selected PATH or CREATE_NEW or empty string
   151→# ──────────────────────────────
   152→_menu_fzf_pick() {
   153→  # Build tab-separated fzf input: PATH\tDISPLAY
   154→  local fzf_entries=""
   155→  fzf_entries+=$'CREATE_NEW\t'"$(t "menu_create_new")"$'\n'
   156→  fzf_entries+=$'CLEANUP\t'"$(t "menu_cleanup")"$'\n'
   157→
   158→  local idx
   159→  for idx in "${!_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"; do
   160→    local path="${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$idx]}"
   161→    local name="${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}"
   162→    local last_used="${_CW_MENU_TIMES[$idx]}"
   163→    local reg="${_CW_MENU_REGISTERED[$idx]}"
   164→
   165→    local time_label=""
   166→    if [[ -n "$last_used" ]]; then
   167→      local rel
   168→      rel=$(_relative_time "$last_used")
   169→      [[ -n "$rel" ]] && time_label="  $(dim "$rel")"
   170→    fi
   171→
   172→    local extra_label=""
   173→    [[ "$reg" == "false" ]] && extra_label+="  $(yellow "[unregistered]")"
   174→    [[ ! -d "$path" ]] && extra_label+="  $(red "[missing]")"
   175→
   176→    fzf_entries+="${path}"$'\t'"${name}${time_label}${extra_label}"$'\n'
   177→  done
   178→
   179→  # Preview script (no bash vars — uses fzf {1} placeholder)
   180→  local preview_script
   181→  preview_script=$(cat <<'PREVIEW'
   182→ws_path={1}
   183→ws_file="${ws_path}/.workspace.json"
   184→if [[ "$ws_path" == "CREATE_NEW" ]]; then
   185→  echo ""
   186→  echo "  Create a new Claude Workspace"
   187→  echo ""
   188→  echo "  A new directory will be created under"
   189→  echo "  ~/WorkingProjects/ and set up for cw."
   190→elif [[ "$ws_path" == "CLEANUP" ]]; then
   191→  echo ""
   192→  echo "  Cleanup registry"
   193→  echo ""
   194→  echo "  Removes entries whose workspace directory"
   195→  echo "  no longer exists on disk."
   196→elif [[ -f "$ws_file" ]]; then
   197→  name=$(jq -r '.name // ""' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   198→  desc=$(jq -r '.description // ""' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   199→  created=$(jq -r '.created_at // ""' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   200→  dir_count=$(jq '.dirs | length' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   201→  echo ""
   202→  echo "  $name"
   203→  [[ -n "$desc" ]] && echo "  $desc"
   204→  echo ""
   205→  echo "  $ws_path"
   206→  echo "  Dirs: $dir_count    Created: ${created:0:10}"
   207→  echo ""
   208→  while IFS= read -r dir_entry; do
   209→    dir_path=$(echo "$dir_entry" | jq -r '.path' 2>/dev/null)
   210→    dir_role=$(echo "$dir_entry" | jq -r '.role // ""' 2>/dev/null)
   211→    label="${dir_role:-$(basename "$dir_path")}"
   212→    if [[ -d "$dir_path" ]]; then
   213→      echo "  ✓  $label"
   214→    else
   215→      echo "  ✗  $label  (missing)"
   216→    fi
   217→  done < <(jq -c '.dirs[]' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   218→else
   219→  echo ""
   220→  echo "  (no .workspace.json found)"
   221→  echo "  $ws_path"
   222→fi
   223→PREVIEW
   224→)
   225→
   226→  local selected
   227→  selected=$(printf '%s' "$fzf_entries" | \
   228→    fzf --ansi \
   229→        --height=70% --border \
   230→        --delimiter=$'\t' \
   231→        --with-nth='2..' \
   232→        --header="  claude-workspace (cw)" \
   233→        --header-first \
   234→        --preview="$preview_script" \
   235→        --preview-window='right:45%:wrap' \
   236→        --prompt='  Workspace > ' \
   237→        2>/dev/null || true)
   238→
   239→  [[ -z "$selected" ]] && echo "" && return 0
   240→
   241→  # Return the PATH (field 1, before the tab)
   242→  printf '%s' "$selected" | cut -f1
   243→}
   244→
   245→# ──────────────────────────────
   246→# Numbered picker (no fzf): returns selected PATH or CREATE_NEW or empty
   247→# ──────────────────────────────
   248→_menu_numbered_pick() {
   249→  echo "" >&2
   250→  echo "$(bold "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")" >&2
   251→  echo "$(bold "  claude-workspace (cw)")" >&2
   252→  echo "$(bold "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")" >&2
   253→
   254→  if [[ ${#_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   255→    echo "" >&2
   256→    info "$(t "workspace_not_found")" >&2
   257→    echo "" >&2
   258→    read -rep "  $(t "new_workspace")? [Y/n]: " ans
   259→    if [[ ! "$ans" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]]; then
   260→      echo "CREATE_NEW"
   261→    else
   262→      echo ""
   263→    fi
   264→    return 0
   265→  fi
   266→
   267→  echo "" >&2
   268→  echo "  $(bold "$(t "menu_recent")")" >&2
   269→  echo "" >&2
   270→
   271→  local i=0
   272→  local idx
   273→  for idx in "${!_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"; do
   274→    local path="${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$idx]}"
   275→    local name="${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}"
   276→    local last_used="${_CW_MENU_TIMES[$idx]}"
   277→    local registered="${_CW_MENU_REGISTERED[$idx]}"
   278→    i=$((i + 1))
   279→
   280→    local status_marker=""
   281→    if [[ ! -d "$path" ]]; then
   282→      status_marker="  $(red "✗ $(t "path_missing")")"
   283→    elif [[ "$path" == "$(pwd)" ]]; then
   284→      status_marker="  $(green "← $(t "current_location")")"
   285→    fi
   286→
   287→    local unreg_label=""
   288→    [[ "$registered" == "false" ]] && unreg_label="  $(yellow "$(t "unregistered")")"
   289→
   290→    local time_label=""
   291→    if [[ -n "$last_used" ]]; then
   292→      local rel
   293→      rel=$(_relative_time "$last_used")
   294→      [[ -n "$rel" ]] && time_label="  $(dim "$rel")"
   295→    fi
   296→
   297→    printf "  [%d] $(bold "%s")%s%s%s\n" "$i" "$name" "$time_label" "$unreg_label" "$status_marker" >&2
   298→    printf "      $(dim "%s")\n" "$path" >&2
   299→    echo "" >&2
   300→  done
   301→
   302→  echo "  $(dim "────────────────────────────────────────")" >&2
   303→  echo "  [N] $(t "menu_create_new")" >&2
   304→  echo "  [C] $(t "menu_cleanup")" >&2
   305→  echo "  [Q] $(t "menu_quit")" >&2
   306→  echo "" >&2
   307→
   308→  local choice
   309→  read -rep "  $(t "cmd_select") [1-${i} / N / C / Q]: " choice
   310→  echo "" >&2
   311→
   312→  case "$choice" in
   313→    [Nn])
   314→      echo "CREATE_NEW"
   315→      ;;
   316→    [Cc])
   317→      echo "CLEANUP"
   318→      ;;
   319→    [Qq]|"")
   320→      echo ""
   321→      ;;
   322→    *)
   323→      if [[ "$choice" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && \
   324→         [[ $choice -ge 1 ]] && \
   325→         [[ $choice -le ${#_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]} ]]; then
   326→        local sel_idx=$(( choice - 1 ))
   327→        echo "${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$sel_idx]}"
   328→      else
   329→        error "$(t "invalid"): $choice" >&2
   330→        echo ""
   331→      fi
   332→      ;;
   333→  esac
   334→}
   335→
   336→# ──────────────────────────────
   337→# Submenu picker: returns action code
   338→# Returns: LAUNCH, ADD_DIR, INFO, FINDER, FORGET, BACK, or empty
   339→# ──────────────────────────────
   340→_menu_submenu_pick() {
   341→  local ws_path="$1"
   342→  local ws_name="$2"
   343→
   344→  local resume_label add_dir_label info_label finder_label forget_label back_label
   345→  resume_label=$(t "menu_action_resume")
   346→  add_dir_label=$(t "menu_action_add_dir")
   347→  info_label=$(t "menu_action_info")
   348→  finder_label=$(t "menu_action_finder")
   349→  forget_label=$(t "menu_action_forget")
   350→  back_label=$(t "menu_action_back")
   351→
   352→  local action
   353→  if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
   354→    action=$(printf '%s\n' \
   355→      "$resume_label" \
   356→      "$add_dir_label" \
   357→      "$info_label" \
   358→      "$finder_label" \
   359→      "$forget_label" \
   360→      "$back_label" | \
   361→      fzf --height=40% --border \
   362→          --header="  $ws_name" \
   363→          --header-first \
   364→          --prompt='  Action > ' \
   365→          2>/dev/null || true)
   366→  else
   367→    # Numbered submenu fallback (no fzf)
   368→    echo "" >&2
   369→    echo "  $(bold "$ws_name")" >&2
   370→    echo "" >&2
   371→    echo "  1) $resume_label" >&2
   372→    echo "  2) $add_dir_label" >&2
   373→    echo "  3) $info_label" >&2
   374→    echo "  4) $finder_label" >&2
   375→    echo "  5) $forget_label" >&2
   376→    echo "  6) $back_label" >&2
   377→    echo "" >&2
   378→    local sub_choice
   379→    read -rep "  Select [1-6]: " sub_choice
   380→    case "$sub_choice" in
   381→      1) action="$resume_label" ;;
   382→      2) action="$add_dir_label" ;;
   383→      3) action="$info_label" ;;
   384→      4) action="$finder_label" ;;
   385→      5) action="$forget_label" ;;
   386→      6) action="$back_label" ;;
   387→      *) action="$back_label" ;;
   388→    esac
   389→  fi
   390→
   391→  if [[ -z "$action" ]] || [[ "$action" == "$back_label" ]]; then
   392→    echo "BACK"
   393→  elif [[ "$action" == "$resume_label" ]]; then
   394→    echo "LAUNCH"
   395→  elif [[ "$action" == "$add_dir_label" ]]; then
   396→    echo "ADD_DIR"
   397→  elif [[ "$action" == "$info_label" ]]; then
   398→    echo "INFO"
   399→  elif [[ "$action" == "$finder_label" ]]; then
   400→    echo "FINDER"
   401→  elif [[ "$action" == "$forget_label" ]]; then
   402→    echo "FORGET"
   403→  else
   404→    echo "BACK"
   405→  fi
   406→}
   407→
   408→# ──────────────────────────────
   409→# Forget workspace (no pwd dependency)
   410→# ──────────────────────────────
   411→_menu_forget() {
   412→  local ws_path="$1"
   413→  local ws_name="$2"
   414→
   415→  # Step 1: Pick mode via fzf (or numbered fallback)
   416→  local registry_only_label delete_dir_label
   417→  registry_only_label=$(t "forget_mode_registry")
   418→  delete_dir_label=$(t "forget_mode_delete")
   419→
   420→  local mode
   421→  if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
   422→    mode=$(printf '%s\n' "$registry_only_label" "$delete_dir_label" | \
   423→      fzf --height=30% --border \
   424→          --header="  $ws_name" \
   425→          --header-first \
   426→          --prompt='  Delete mode > ' \
   427→          2>/dev/null || true)
   428→  else
   429→    # Numbered fallback (output to stderr to avoid stdout pollution)
   430→    echo "" >&2
   431→    echo "  1) $registry_only_label" >&2
   432→    echo "  2) $delete_dir_label" >&2
   433→    echo "" >&2
   434→    local choice
   435→    read -rp "  Select [1/2]: " choice
   436→    case "$choice" in
   437→      1) mode="$registry_only_label" ;;
   438→      2) mode="$delete_dir_label" ;;
   439→      *) mode="" ;;
   440→    esac
   441→  fi
   442→
   443→  [[ -z "$mode" ]] && return  # Esc -> back to sub-menu
   444→
   445→  if [[ "$mode" == "$registry_only_label" ]]; then
   446→    # Registry only (current behavior)
   447→    echo ""
   448→    warn "\"${ws_name}\" $(t "remove_from_registry")"
   449→    echo "  $(dim "$(t "files_remain")")"
   450→    echo ""
   451→    read -rp "  $(t "continue") [y/N]: " confirm
   452→    if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
   453→      registry_remove "$ws_path"
   454→      success "$(t "deleted"): $ws_name"
   455→      echo ""
   456→    else
   457→      info "$(t "cancel")"
   458→    fi
   459→
   460→  elif [[ "$mode" == "$delete_dir_label" ]]; then
   461→    # Safety check: must be under WorkingProjects/
   462→    local wp_base="${WORKING_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/WorkingProjects}"
   463→    wp_base=$(expand_path "$wp_base")
   464→    if [[ "$ws_path" != "$wp_base"/* ]]; then
   465→      echo ""
   466→      error "$(t "forget_delete_outside_wp")"
   467→      echo "  $(dim "$ws_path")"
   468→      echo ""
   469→      return  # back to sub-menu
   470→    fi
   471→
   472→    # Destructive confirmation
   473→    echo ""
   474→    warn "$(t "forget_delete_confirm"): \"$ws_name\""
   475→    echo "  $(dim "$ws_path")"
   476→    warn "$(t "irreversible")"
   477→    echo ""
   478→    read -rp "  $(t "continue") [y/N]: " confirm
   479→    if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
   480→      registry_remove "$ws_path"
   481→      rm -rf "$ws_path"
   482→      success "$(t "forget_dir_deleted"): $ws_name"
   483→      echo ""
   484→    else
   485→      info "$(t "cancel")"
   486→    fi
   487→  fi
   488→}
   489→
   490→# ──────────────────────────────
   491→# Cleanup registry: remove entries for missing directories
   492→# ──────────────────────────────
   493→_menu_cleanup() {
   494→  local missing_paths=()
   495→  local missing_names=()
   496→
   497→  local ws_list
   498→  ws_list=$(registry_list)
   499→
   500→  while IFS= read -r ws; do
   501→    local name path
   502→    name=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.name')
   503→    path=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.path')
   504→    if [[ ! -d "$path" ]]; then
   505→      missing_paths+=("$path")
   506→      missing_names+=("$name")
   507→    fi
   508→  done < <(echo "$ws_list" | jq -c '.[]' 2>/dev/null)
   509→
   510→  if [[ ${#missing_paths[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   511→    echo ""
   512→    info "$(t "cleanup_none")"
   513→    echo ""
   514→    read -rp "  $(t "press_enter")" _
   515→    return
   516→  fi
   517→
   518→  echo ""
   519→  warn "${#missing_paths[@]} $(t "cleanup_found")"
   520→  echo ""
   521→  local i
   522→  for i in "${!missing_paths[@]}"; do
   523→    echo "  $(red "✗")  ${missing_names[$i]}  $(dim "${missing_paths[$i]}")"
   524→  done
   525→  echo ""
   526→  read -rp "  $(t "continue") [y/N]: " confirm
   527→  if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
   528→    local path
   529→    for path in "${missing_paths[@]+"${missing_paths[@]}"}"; do
   530→      registry_remove "$path"
   531→    done
   532→    success "${#missing_paths[@]} $(t "cleanup_done")"
   533→    echo ""
   534→  else
   535→    info "$(t "cancel")"
   536→  fi
   537→}
   538→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# new.sh - cw new command
     3→# Creates a new Workspace folder under WorkingProjects/ and sets it up
     4→
     5→cmd_new() {
     6→  require_jq
     7→
     8→  # Normalize WORKING_PROJECTS_DIR to absolute path
     9→  local raw_wp="${WORKING_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/WorkingProjects}"
    10→  local wp_dir
    11→  wp_dir=$(expand_path "$raw_wp")
    12→
    13→  echo ""
    14→  echo "$(bold "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
    15→  echo "$(bold "  $(t "new_workspace")")"
    16→  echo "$(bold "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
    17→  echo ""
    18→
    19→  # ──────────────────────────────
    20→  # Determine Workspace name
    21→  # ──────────────────────────────
    22→  local ws_name="${1:-}"
    23→
    24→  if [[ -z "$ws_name" ]]; then
    25→    read -rep "  $(t "workspace_name"): " ws_name
    26→    ws_name="${ws_name%"${ws_name##*[![:space:]]}"}"  # trim trailing spaces
    27→    ws_name="${ws_name#"${ws_name%%[![:space:]]*}"}"  # trim leading spaces
    28→    if [[ -z "$ws_name" ]]; then
    29→      error "$(t "workspace_name") $(t "required")"
    30→      exit 1
    31→    fi
    32→  fi
    33→
    34→  # ──────────────────────────────
    35→  # Generate folder name
    36→  # Spaces -> hyphens, remove leading/trailing hyphens
    37→  # ──────────────────────────────
    38→  local folder_name
    39→  folder_name=$(echo "$ws_name" | tr '/ \\:*?' '-' | sed 's/^-*//;s/-*$//' | sed 's/-\{2,\}/-/g')
    40→
    41→  local target_dir="$wp_dir/$folder_name"
    42→
    43→  echo ""
    44→  echo "  $(bold "$(t "workspace_name")"):  $ws_name"
    45→  echo "  $(bold "$(t "directory")"):       $target_dir"
    46→  echo ""
    47→
    48→  # Already exists
    49→  if [[ -d "$target_dir" ]]; then
    50→    if is_workspace "$target_dir"; then
    51→      warn "$(t "already_exists")"
    52→      read -rep "  Launch as is? [Y/n]: " ans
    53→      if [[ ! "$ans" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]]; then
    54→        cd "$target_dir"
    55→        cmd_launch
    56→      fi
    57→      return
    58→    else
    59→      warn "$(t "directory") $(t "already_exists"): $target_dir"
    60→      read -rep "  $(t "workspace_setup")? [Y/n]: " ans
    61→      [[ "$ans" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]] && { info "$(t "cancel")"; exit 0; }
    62→    fi
    63→  else
    64→    read -rep "  $(t "create") [Y/n]: " confirm
    65→    [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]] && { info "$(t "cancel")"; exit 0; }
    66→
    67→    mkdir -p "$target_dir"
    68→    success "$(t "created"): $target_dir"
    69→  fi
    70→
    71→  # ──────────────────────────────
    72→  # Move to created directory and run setup
    73→  # (pass ws_name to skip name input)
    74→  # ──────────────────────────────
    75→  cd "$target_dir"
    76→  cmd_setup "$ws_name"
    77→}
    78→

content.text:
必要な情報が揃いました。レビューを行います。

---

## APPROVE

判定: **APPROVE（軽微な指摘あり）**

---

### 各基準スコア

| 基準 | スコア | 評価 |
|------|--------|------|
| Clarity（明確さ） | 4/5 | 全体的に明確。一部に実装者の裁量が残る箇所あり |
| Verifiability（検証可能性） | 5/5 | Success Criteria が具体的で検証可能 |
| Completeness（完全性） | 4/5 | 必要な情報はほぼ揃っている |
| Consistency（一貫性） | 5/5 | 内部矛盾なし。既存コードとの整合性も取れている |

---

### 総評

仕様書の品質は高く、実装に着手できる状態です。以下のポイントが特に良くできています。

- `gum_choose` が内部定数（`LAUNCH`/`CREATE`/`CANCEL`）を返す設計を明示し、i18n 翻訳値と分離している点
- `cmd_launch` への `[dir]` 引数追加の根拠（`pwd` 依存を排除する理由）が明文化されている点
- `cw new` が Phase 3 の launch 判定を `cmd_setup` に委任し、`cmd_new` 側で二重呼び出しを行わないことが明示されている点
- フォールバック表が関数ごとに1対1で対応している点

---

### 軽微な指摘（実装前に確認を推奨）

**1. `gum_spin` のフォールバック時のエラー伝播（Clarity 軽微）**

仕様には「direct execution fallback」と記載されているが、`gum spin` はラップするコマンドが失敗したとき終了コードを伝播する。フォールバック実装でも同様に終了コードを伝播させるかどうかが未明記。実装者が当然そうするとは思われるが、`set -euo pipefail` 環境下での挙動として一行明示しておくと安全。

**2. Phase 2 の「Esc を押す」終了方法（Clarity 軽微）**

「Loop continues until user presses Esc or submits empty input」と記載されているが、`gum input` はフォールバックの `read -rep` と Esc 挙動が異なる（`gum input` は Esc で空文字列を返す vs `read` は Ctrl+D/空 Enter）。フォールバック環境での「ループ終了トリガー」が暗黙的になっており、仕様書に「空 Enter でループ終了（両環境共通）」と補足があると実装者の誤解を防げる。

**3. `cmd_launch` の引数インターフェース変更の影響範囲（Completeness 軽微）**

仕様書では `lib/launch.sh` の変更として「オプション `[dir]` 引数を追加する」と記載されている。既存コードを確認すると、`cmd_launch` は現在 `$1` をワークスペース名として使用している（`_find_workspace_by_name "$1"`）。新仕様ではこの `$1` をパス直接渡しに変更するか、フラグで区別するかを仕様書が明示していない。呼び出し元（`menu.sh` の `cmd_launch`）との整合性に影響するため、シグネチャ変更の具体的方針（例: `cmd_launch [--dir <path>]` or `cmd_launch [name_or_path]`）を明示することを推奨。

---

指摘事項はすべて軽微であり、実装を開始するにあたって仕様が不完全であるとは判断しません。指摘 3 については実装開始前に担当者と軽く合意しておくことを推奨します。

content.content: Launching skill: superpowers:writing-plans

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/yurikamo/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.2/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans — one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Plan Review Loop

After completing each chunk of the plan:

1. Dispatch plan-document-reviewer subagent (see plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) with precisely crafted review context — never your session history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the plan, not your thought process.
   - Provide: chunk content, path to spec document
2. If ❌ Issues Found:
   - Fix the issues in the chunk
   - Re-dispatch reviewer for that chunk
   - Repeat until ✅ Approved
3. If ✅ Approved: proceed to next chunk (or execution handoff if last chunk)

**Chunk boundaries:** Use `## Chunk N: <name>` headings to delimit chunks. Each chunk should be ≤1000 lines and logically self-contained.

**Review loop guidance:**
- Same agent that wrote the plan fixes it (preserves context)
- If loop exceeds 5 iterations, surface to human for guidance
- Reviewers are advisory - explain disagreements if you believe feedback is incorrect

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Ready to execute?"**

**Execution path depends on harness capabilities:**

**If harness has subagents (Claude Code, etc.):**
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Do NOT offer a choice - subagent-driven is the standard approach
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If harness does NOT have subagents:**
- Execute plan in current session using superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

実装計画を作成します。

まず仕様書を再確認してから、計画を書きます。

content.content:
1→# Design: Interactive Wizard UI (gum-based)
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-03-13
     4→**Status:** Approved
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Overview
     9→
    10→Redesign the `cw setup` / `cw new` flow from a linear 7-step prompt sequence into a 3-phase interactive wizard using [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum). Apply gum-based UI components across all commands for consistent, visually rich interactions. Maintain a fallback to the existing `read`-based UI when gum is not installed.
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## Problem Statement
    15→
    16→Current pain points identified:
    17→
    18→1. **Directory selection is repetitive** — adding directories one by one with no visible running list makes the flow confusing.
    19→2. **7-step sequence feels long** — no visible progress indicator; users don't know how far along they are.
    20→3. **Weak input validation** — invalid paths are not flagged immediately at input time.
    21→4. **Unclear next action after completion** — setup ends with "run `cw`" but does not offer to launch automatically.
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Solution
    26→
    27→### Core: 3-Phase Wizard (`cw setup` / `cw new`)
    28→
    29→Replace the 7-step linear flow with 3 named phases:
    30→
    31→#### Phase 1 — Basic Info
    32→- `gum input` for workspace name (pre-filled with directory basename for `cw setup`)
    33→- `gum input` for description (optional)
    34→- Phase header shows `Phase 1/3: Basic Info`
    35→
    36→#### Phase 2 — Directories
    37→- Display a live "Added so far" list before each prompt
    38→- Path input uses `gum_path_input` (see Architecture) with the following tool-combination behavior:
    39→  - gum + fzf both available: fzf for directory browsing (gum used for styling the prompt header only)
    40→  - gum only: `gum input` (type path directly; no Tab-fzf integration — not feasible with gum input)
    41→  - fzf only: `select_dir_with_fzf` (existing behavior)
    42→  - neither: `read -rep` fallback
    43→- Immediate validation: if path does not exist, show colored warning and `gum confirm "Add anyway?"` (or `[y/N]` fallback)
    44→- `gum input` for optional role label per directory
    45→- Loop continues until user presses Esc or submits empty input
    46→- At least one directory is not required (user can add later with `cw add-dir`)
    47→
    48→#### Phase 3 — Confirm & Launch
    49→- Summary box showing: name, description, directory count, workspace path, files to be created
    50→- `gum_choose` with three options (comparison uses internal constants, not i18n values):
    51→  - `LAUNCH` → display label `t "phase3_opt_launch"` — create workspace files then call `cmd_launch` with explicit `target_dir`
    52→  - `CREATE` → display label `t "phase3_opt_create"` — create files and exit (existing behavior)
    53→  - `CANCEL` → display label `t "phase3_opt_cancel"` — abort without writing anything
    54→- `gum_choose` returns the internal constant (e.g. `LAUNCH`); Phase 3 uses `case "$choice" in LAUNCH) ...` for branching
    55→- When "LAUNCH" is chosen, `cmd_launch` is called with the workspace directory passed explicitly (not relying on `pwd`)
    56→
    57→### Global UI Upgrades (all commands)
    58→
    59→| Current pattern | Replacement |
    60→|----------------|-------------|
    61→| `read -rep "... [y/N]: "` | `gum confirm "..."` |
    62→| Processing steps (file write, skill install) | `gum spin --title "..."` wrapping the operation |
    63→| `warn "..."` / `error "..."` output | `gum style` with foreground color + border for errors |
    64→| `cw add-dir` path prompt | `gum input` + fzf, same validation as Phase 2 |
    65→| `cw forget` confirmation | `gum confirm` with destructive-action styling |
    66→
    67→---
    68→
    69→## Architecture
    70→
    71→### New file: `lib/gum.sh`
    72→
    73→Central gum abstraction layer. Provides wrapper functions that:
    74→- Call gum when available (`command -v gum`)
    75→- Fall back to existing `read`/`echo` patterns otherwise
    76→
    77→Key functions:
    78→```
    79→gum_input <prompt> [default]      → gum input / read fallback
    80→gum_confirm <message>             → gum confirm / [y/N] fallback
    81→gum_spin <title> <command...>     → gum spin / direct execution fallback
    82→                                     (bash 3.2 safe: uses "${@:2}" for command args)
    83→gum_error <message>               → gum style (red border) / warn fallback
    84→gum_choose <options...>           → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
    85→                                     (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
    86→gum_path_input <prompt> [default] → tool-combination-aware path selector:
    87→                                     gum+fzf: fzf browse (gum for prompt styling only)
    88→                                     gum only: gum input (type path directly)
    89→                                     fzf only: select_dir_with_fzf (existing)
    90→                                     neither:  read -rep fallback
    91→```
    92→
    93→### Modified file: `lib/setup.sh`
    94→
    95→- Replace 7-step sequence with `_setup_phase1`, `_setup_phase2`, `_setup_phase3` internal functions
    96→- Call gum wrapper functions throughout
    97→- Phase 3 result drives post-setup action (launch or exit)
    98→- When "LAUNCH" is chosen, call `cmd_launch` with `target_dir` passed explicitly — do NOT rely on `pwd` (bash's `pwd` state is not guaranteed to match `target_dir` after subshell calls)
    99→- `cmd_launch` will need to accept an optional `[dir]` argument; if provided, it `cd`s to that dir instead of using `pwd`
   100→- `cw new` calls `cmd_setup` and then returns; Phase 3 inside `cmd_setup` handles the launch decision, so `cmd_new` does **not** add a separate `cmd_launch` call
   101→
   102→### Modified file: `lib/utils.sh` / other command files
   103→
   104→- Replace `read -rep "... [y/N]"` patterns with `gum_confirm`
   105→- Replace processing steps with `gum_spin` wrappers where applicable
   106→
   107→### Modified file: `install.sh`
   108→
   109→- Add gum availability check after jq check
   110→- If gum not found: suggest `brew install gum`, offer to install, or warn and continue
   111→- Document gum as optional (graceful degradation)
   112→
   113→---
   114→
   115→## Fallback Behavior
   116→
   117→When gum is not installed:
   118→- `gum_input` → `read -rep`
   119→- `gum_confirm` → `read -rep "... [y/N]: "`
   120→- `gum_spin` → direct execution (no spinner)
   121→- `gum_error` → existing `warn`/`error` functions
   122→- `gum_choose` → numbered menu (existing `_menu_numbered_pick` pattern)
   123→
   124→All existing behavior is preserved. The wrapper layer in `lib/gum.sh` is the single point of fallback logic.
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→## Out of Scope
   129→
   130→- Full TUI dashboard (panel-based, real-time) — not in this iteration
   131→- `cw web` UI changes — web UI is separate
   132→- `cw list` / `cw info` output styling — low priority, can follow in a future iteration
   133→- Windows/WSL support — not a stated requirement
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Success Criteria
   138→
   139→- `cw setup` completes in 3 clear phases with visible phase indicator
   140→- Directory list is visible throughout Phase 2
   141→- Invalid paths show immediate colored warning
   142→- Phase 3 offers "Create & Launch" that exits directly into Claude Code
   143→- All `[y/N]` prompts replaced with `gum confirm` (when gum installed)
   144→- Processing operations show spinner (when gum installed)
   145→- Full functionality preserved when gum is not installed
   146→- `install.sh` checks for gum and guides the user to install it
   147→
   148→---
   149→
   150→## Dependencies
   151→
   152→- [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum) — optional, `brew install gum`
   153→- No other new dependencies
   154→
   155→---
   156→
   157→## i18n Keys (New)
   158→
   159→All new user-facing strings must be added to both `en` and `ja` blocks in `lib/i18n.sh`. Confirmed additions:
   160→
   161→| Key | English | Japanese |
   162→|-----|---------|---------|
   163→| `phase1_header` | `Phase 1/3: Basic Info` | `フェーズ 1/3: 基本情報` |
   164→| `phase2_header` | `Phase 2/3: Directories` | `フェーズ 2/3: ディレクトリ` |
   165→| `phase3_header` | `Phase 3/3: Confirm` | `フェーズ 3/3: 確認` |
   166→| `phase3_opt_launch` | `Create & Launch Claude` | `作成して Claude を起動` |
   167→| `phase3_opt_create` | `Create only` | `作成のみ` |
   168→| `phase3_opt_cancel` | `Cancel` | `キャンセル` |
   169→| `phase3_summary` | `Summary` | `内容確認` |
   170→| `path_add_anyway` | `Path does not exist. Add anyway?` | `ディレクトリが存在しません。追加しますか?` |
   171→| `dirs_added_so_far` | `Added so far:` | `追加済み:` |
   172→| `gum_required_hint` | `Install gum for a richer UI: brew install gum` | `より良いUIのために gum をインストール: brew install gum` |
   173→
   174→All Phase 1–3 display strings must go through `t "key"`. No hardcoded display strings allowed.
   175→
   176→---
   177→
   178→## Files to Create / Modify
   179→
   180→| File | Action |
   181→|------|--------|
   182→| `lib/gum.sh` | Create — gum wrapper abstraction |
   183→| `lib/setup.sh` | Modify — rewrite to 3-phase wizard |
   184→| `lib/utils.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
   185→| `lib/menu.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
   186→| `lib/new.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |

file.content:
# Design: Interactive Wizard UI (gum-based)

**Date:** 2026-03-13
**Status:** Approved

---

## Overview

Redesign the `cw setup` / `cw new` flow from a linear 7-step prompt sequence into a 3-phase interactive wizard using [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum). Apply gum-based UI components across all commands for consistent, visually rich interactions. Maintain a fallback to the existing `read`-based UI when gum is not installed.

---

## Problem Statement

Current pain points identified:

1. **Directory selection is repetitive** — adding directories one by one with no visible running list makes the flow confusing.
2. **7-step sequence feels long** — no visible progress indicator; users don't know how far along they are.
3. **Weak input validation** — invalid paths are not flagged immediately at input time.
4. **Unclear next action after completion** — setup ends with "run `cw`" but does not offer to launch automatically.

---

## Solution

### Core: 3-Phase Wizard (`cw setup` / `cw new`)

Replace the 7-step linear flow with 3 named phases:

#### Phase 1 — Basic Info
- `gum input` for workspace name (pre-filled with directory basename for `cw setup`)
- `gum input` for description (optional)
- Phase header shows `Phase 1/3: Basic Info`

#### Phase 2 — Directories
- Display a live "Added so far" list before each prompt
- Path input uses `gum_path_input` (see Architecture) with the following tool-combination behavior:
  - gum + fzf both available: fzf for directory browsing (gum used for styling the prompt header only)
  - gum only: `gum input` (type path directly; no Tab-fzf integration — not feasible with gum input)
  - fzf only: `select_dir_with_fzf` (existing behavior)
  - neither: `read -rep` fallback
- Immediate validation: if path does not exist, show colored warning and `gum confirm "Add anyway?"` (or `[y/N]` fallback)
- `gum input` for optional role label per directory
- Loop continues until user presses Esc or submits empty input
- At least one directory is not required (user can add later with `cw add-dir`)

#### Phase 3 — Confirm & Launch
- Summary box showing: name, description, directory count, workspace path, files to be created
- `gum_choose` with three options (comparison uses internal constants, not i18n values):
  - `LAUNCH` → display label `t "phase3_opt_launch"` — create workspace files then call `cmd_launch` with explicit `target_dir`
  - `CREATE` → display label `t "phase3_opt_create"` — create files and exit (existing behavior)
  - `CANCEL` → display label `t "phase3_opt_cancel"` — abort without writing anything
- `gum_choose` returns the internal constant (e.g. `LAUNCH`); Phase 3 uses `case "$choice" in LAUNCH) ...` for branching
- When "LAUNCH" is chosen, `cmd_launch` is called with the workspace directory passed explicitly (not relying on `pwd`)

### Global UI Upgrades (all commands)

| Current pattern | Replacement |
|----------------|-------------|
| `read -rep "... [y/N]: "` | `gum confirm "..."` |
| Processing steps (file write, skill install) | `gum spin --title "..."` wrapping the operation |
| `warn "..."` / `error "..."` output | `gum style` with foreground color + border for errors |
| `cw add-dir` path prompt | `gum input` + fzf, same validation as Phase 2 |
| `cw forget` confirmation | `gum confirm` with destructive-action styling |

---

## Architecture

### New file: `lib/gum.sh`

Central gum abstraction layer. Provides wrapper functions that:
- Call gum when available (`command -v gum`)
- Fall back to existing `read`/`echo` patterns otherwise

Key functions:
```
gum_input <prompt> [default]      → gum input / read fallback
gum_confirm <message>             → gum confirm / [y/N] fallback
gum_spin <title> <command...>     → gum spin / direct execution fallback
                                     (bash 3.2 safe: uses "${@:2}" for command args)
gum_error <message>               → gum style (red border) / warn fallback
gum_choose <options...>           → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                                     (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
gum_path_input <prompt> [default] → tool-combination-aware path selector:
                                     gum+fzf: fzf browse (gum for prompt styling only)
                                     gum only: gum input (type path directly)
                                     fzf only: select_dir_with_fzf (existing)
                                     neither:  read -rep fallback
```

### Modified file: `lib/setup.sh`

- Replace 7-step sequence with `_setup_phase1`, `_setup_phase2`, `_setup_phase3` internal functions
- Call gum wrapper functions throughout
- Phase 3 result drives post-setup action (launch or exit)
- When "LAUNCH" is chosen, call `cmd_launch` with `target_dir` passed explicitly — do NOT rely on `pwd` (bash's `pwd` state is not guaranteed to match `target_dir` after subshell calls)
- `cmd_launch` will need to accept an optional `[dir]` argument; if provided, it `cd`s to that dir instead of using `pwd`
- `cw new` calls `cmd_setup` and then returns; Phase 3 inside `cmd_setup` handles the launch decision, so `cmd_new` does **not** add a separate `cmd_launch` call

### Modified file: `lib/utils.sh` / other command files

- Replace `read -rep "... [y/N]"` patterns with `gum_confirm`
- Replace processing steps with `gum_spin` wrappers where applicable

### Modified file: `install.sh`

- Add gum availability check after jq check
- If gum not found: suggest `brew install gum`, offer to install, or warn and continue
- Document gum as optional (graceful degradation)

---

## Fallback Behavior

When gum is not installed:
- `gum_input` → `read -rep`
- `gum_confirm` → `read -rep "... [y/N]: "`
- `gum_spin` → direct execution (no spinner)
- `gum_error` → existing `warn`/`error` functions
- `gum_choose` → numbered menu (existing `_menu_numbered_pick` pattern)

All existing behavior is preserved. The wrapper layer in `lib/gum.sh` is the single point of fallback logic.

---

## Out of Scope

- Full TUI dashboard (panel-based, real-time) — not in this iteration
- `cw web` UI changes — web UI is separate
- `cw list` / `cw info` output styling — low priority, can follow in a future iteration
- Windows/WSL support — not a stated requirement

---

## Success Criteria

- `cw setup` completes in 3 clear phases with visible phase indicator
- Directory list is visible throughout Phase 2
- Invalid paths show immediate colored warning
- Phase 3 offers "Create & Launch" that exits directly into Claude Code
- All `[y/N]` prompts replaced with `gum confirm` (when gum installed)
- Processing operations show spinner (when gum installed)
- Full functionality preserved when gum is not installed
- `install.sh` checks for gum and guides the user to install it

---

## Dependencies

- [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum) — optional, `brew install gum`
- No other new dependencies

---

## i18n Keys (New)

All new user-facing strings must be added to both `en` and `ja` blocks in `lib/i18n.sh`. Confirmed additions:

| Key | English | Japanese |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `phase1_header` | `Phase 1/3: Basic Info` | `フェーズ 1/3: 基本情報` |
| `phase2_header` | `Phase 2/3: Directories` | `フェーズ 2/3: ディレクトリ` |
| `phase3_header` | `Phase 3/3: Confirm` | `フェーズ 3/3: 確認` |
| `phase3_opt_launch` | `Create & Launch Claude` | `作成して Claude を起動` |
| `phase3_opt_create` | `Create only` | `作成のみ` |
| `phase3_opt_cancel` | `Cancel` | `キャンセル` |
| `phase3_summary` | `Summary` | `内容確認` |
| `path_add_anyway` | `Path does not exist. Add anyway?` | `ディレクトリが存在しません。追加しますか?` |
| `dirs_added_so_far` | `Added so far:` | `追加済み:` |
| `gum_required_hint` | `Install gum for a richer UI: brew install gum` | `より良いUIのために gum をインストール: brew install gum` |

All Phase 1–3 display strings must go through `t "key"`. No hardcoded display strings allowed.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `lib/gum.sh` | Create — gum wrapper abstraction |
| `lib/setup.sh` | Modify — rewrite to 3-phase wizard |
| `lib/utils.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/menu.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/new.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/launch.sh` | Modify — add optional `[dir]` argument to `cmd_launch` for Phase 3 direct call |
| `install.sh` | Modify — add gum availability check |
| `lib/i18n.sh` | Modify — add all keys listed in i18n Keys section above |

input.content:
# Interactive Wizard UI (gum-based) Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Replace `cw setup`/`cw new` with a 3-phase interactive wizard using gum, and apply gum UI components globally with graceful fallback when gum is not installed.

**Architecture:** A new `lib/gum.sh` provides all gum wrapper functions with fallbacks. `lib/setup.sh` is rewritten into three phase functions. `lib/launch.sh` gains an optional directory argument. All `[y/N]` prompts across commands are replaced with `gum_confirm`.

**Tech Stack:** Bash 3.2+ (macOS compatible), [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum) (optional), fzf (optional), jq (required)

---

## Chunk 1: Foundation

### Task 1: Create `lib/gum.sh` — gum wrapper abstraction

**Files:**
- Create: `lib/gum.sh`

**Context:** This is the single abstraction layer for all gum UI. When gum is not installed, every function falls back to plain bash. Regular arrays (`local arr=(...)`) work in bash 3.2; associative arrays and `${var,,}` do NOT.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `lib/gum.sh` with all wrapper functions**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# gum.sh - gum wrapper abstraction layer
# All TUI components with graceful fallback when gum is not installed

_gum_available() { command -v gum &>/dev/null; }
_fzf_available()  { command -v fzf  &>/dev/null; }

# ──────────────────────────────
# gum_input <prompt> [default]
# Interactive text input; echoes result to stdout
# ──────────────────────────────
gum_input() {
  local prompt="$1"
  local default="${2:-}"

  if _gum_available; then
    gum input --prompt "  ${prompt}: " --placeholder "${default}" --value "${default}"
  else
    local input
    if [[ -n "$default" ]]; then
      read -rep "  ${prompt} [${default}]: " input
      echo "${input:-$default}"
    else
      read -rep "  ${prompt}: " input
      echo "$input"
    fi
  fi
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# gum_confirm <message>
# Returns 0 for yes, 1 for no
# ──────────────────────────────
gum_confirm() {
  local message="$1"

  if _gum_available; then
    gum confirm "  ${message}"
  else
    local answer
    read -rep "  ${message} [y/N]: " answer
    [[ "$answer" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]
  fi
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# gum_spin <title> <command> [args...]
# Run command with spinner; exit code is propagated
# bash 3.2 safe: shift leaves "$@" as the command
# ──────────────────────────────
gum_spin() {
  local title="$1"
  shift

  if _gum_available; then
    gum spin --title "  ${title}" -- "$@"
  else
    "$@"
  fi
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# gum_error <message>
# Display error with red box styling
# ──────────────────────────────
gum_error() {
  local message="$1"

  if _gum_available; then
    gum style \
      --foreground 9 \
      --border normal \
      --border-foreground 9 \
      --padding "0 1" \
      "  ✗ ${message}" >&2
  else
    error "$message"
  fi
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# gum_choose <label1> <value1> [<label2> <value2> ...]
# Display selection menu; echoes the VALUE of chosen option to stdout
# Args are interleaved label/value pairs
# ──────────────────────────────
gum_choose() {
  local all_args=("$@")
  local pair_count=$(( ${#all_args[@]} / 2 ))

  if _gum_available; then
    # Build label list
    local label_list=""
    local i=0
    while [[ $i -lt $pair_count ]]; do
      local label="${all_args[$(( i * 2 ))]}"
      label_list="${label_list}${label}"$'\n'
      i=$(( i + 1 ))
    done

    local chosen
    chosen=$(printf '%s' "$label_list" | gum choose 2>/dev/null || true)
    [[ -z "$chosen" ]] && return 1

    # Find matching value
    local i=0
    while [[ $i -lt $pair_count ]]; do
      local label="${all_args[$(( i * 2 ))]}"
      local value="${all_args[$(( i * 2 + 1 ))]}"
      if [[ "$label" == "$chosen" ]]; then
        echo "$value"
        return 0
      fi
      i=$(( i + 1 ))
    done

  else
    # Numbered fallback
    echo "" >&2
    local i=0
    while [[ $i -lt $pair_count ]]; do
      local label="${all_args[$(( i * 2 ))]}"
      echo "  [$(( i + 1 ))] $label" >&2
      i=$(( i + 1 ))
    done
    echo "" >&2
    local choice
    read -rep "  $(t "cmd_select") [1-${pair_count}]: " choice
    if [[ "$choice" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && \
       [[ $choice -ge 1 ]] && \
       [[ $choice -le $pair_count ]]; then
      local value="${all_args[$(( (choice - 1) * 2 + 1 ))]}"
      echo "$value"
      return 0
    fi
  fi

  return 1
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# gum_path_input <prompt> [default]
# Directory path selector; tool-combination aware
# gum+fzf: fzf browse (gum for prompt styling only)
# gum only: gum input (type path directly)
# fzf only: select_dir_with_fzf (existing)
# neither:  read -rep fallback
# ──────────────────────────────
gum_path_input() {
  local prompt="$1"
  local default="${2:-}"

  if _gum_available && _fzf_available; then
    # fzf for browsing
    local result
    result=$(
      find "$HOME" -maxdepth 4 -type d \
        \( -path "$HOME/Library" -o -name ".git" -o -name "node_modules" \
           -o -name ".cache" -o -name "__pycache__" \) -prune \
        -o -type d -print 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --height=40% --border \
            --prompt="  ${prompt} > " \
            --preview="ls {}" \
            --preview-window=right:40% \
        2>/dev/null || true
    )
    [[ -n "$result" ]] && expand_path "$result" || echo ""

  elif _gum_available; then
    local raw
    raw=$(gum_input "$prompt" "$default")
    [[ -n "$raw" ]] && expand_path "$raw" || echo ""

  else
    # existing helper from utils.sh
    select_dir_with_fzf "$prompt"
  fi
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the file has no syntax errors**

```bash
bash -n /Users/yurikamo/Developer/github/claude-workspace/lib/gum.sh
```
Expected: no output (no errors)

- [ ] **Step 3: Smoke-test fallback behavior (without gum)**

```bash
cd /Users/yurikamo/Developer/github/claude-workspace
# Source dependencies then gum.sh
bash -c '
  source lib/utils.sh
  source lib/i18n.sh
  source lib/gum.sh
  # Confirm fallback path is taken when gum absent
  PATH_WITHOUT_GUM=$(echo "$PATH" | tr ":" "\n" | grep -v gum | tr "\n" ":")
  export PATH="$PATH_WITHOUT_GUM"
  _gum_available && echo "FAIL: gum should not be found" || echo "PASS: gum unavailable"
'
```
Expected: `PASS: gum unavailable`

- [ ] **Step 4: Add `source "$CW_LIB_DIR/gum.sh"` to `bin/cw` after `utils.sh`**

In `bin/cw`, after line `source "$CW_LIB_DIR/utils.sh"`, add:
```bash
source "$CW_LIB_DIR/gum.sh"
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify bin/cw loads without error**

```bash
bash -n /Users/yurikamo/Developer/github/claude-workspace/bin/cw
```
Expected: no output

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add lib/gum.sh bin/cw
git commit -m "feat(ui): add gum wrapper abstraction layer (lib/gum.sh)"
```

---

### Task 2: Add i18n keys to `lib/i18n.sh`

**Files:**
- Modify: `lib/i18n.sh`

**Context:** All new display strings must go through `t "key"`. The file has two `case` blocks — one for `ja)` and one for the English default. Both must be updated. New keys belong in the `# === Setup ===` section.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add new keys to the `ja)` block in `lib/i18n.sh`**

In the `ja)` case, inside `# === Setup ===`, add after the existing setup keys:
```bash
        "phase1_header") echo "フェーズ 1/3: 基本情報" ;;
        "phase2_header") echo "フェーズ 2/3: ディレクトリ" ;;
        "phase3_header") echo "フェーズ 3/3: 確認" ;;
        "phase3_opt_launch") echo "作成して Claude を起動" ;;
        "phase3_opt_create") echo "作成のみ" ;;
        "phase3_opt_cancel") echo "キャンセル" ;;
        "phase3_summary") echo "内容確認" ;;
        "path_add_anyway") echo "ディレクトリが存在しません。追加しますか?" ;;
        "dirs_added_so_far") echo "追加済み:" ;;
        "gum_required_hint") echo "より良いUIのために gum をインストール: brew install gum" ;;
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add new keys to the English default block**

In the English `*)` case, inside `# === Setup ===`, add after the existing setup keys:
```bash
        "phase1_header") echo "Phase 1/3: Basic Info" ;;
        "phase2_header") echo "Phase 2/3: Directories" ;;
        "phase3_header") echo "Phase 3/3: Confirm" ;;
        "phase3_opt_launch") echo "Create & Launch Claude" ;;
        "phase3_opt_create") echo "Create only" ;;
        "phase3_opt_cancel") echo "Cancel" ;;
        "phase3_summary") echo "Summary" ;;
        "path_add_anyway") echo "Path does not exist. Add anyway?" ;;
        "dirs_added_so_far") echo "Added so far:" ;;
        "gum_required_hint") echo "Install gum for a richer UI: brew install gum" ;;
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify all new keys resolve**

```bash
cd /Users/yurikamo/Developer/github/claude-workspace
bash -c '
  source lib/utils.sh
  source lib/i18n.sh
  for key in phase1_header phase2_header phase3_header phase3_opt_launch \
              phase3_opt_create phase3_opt_cancel phase3_summary \
              path_add_anyway dirs_added_so_far gum_required_hint; do
    val=$(t "$key")
    if [[ "$val" == "$key" ]]; then
      echo "FAIL: key \"$key\" not found (returned key itself)"
    else
      echo "PASS: $key = $val"
    fi
  done
'
```
Expected: 10 lines all starting with `PASS:`

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add lib/i18n.sh
git commit -m "feat(i18n): add wizard phase and gum i18n keys"
```

---

### Task 3: Modify `lib/launch.sh` — add optional `[dir]` argument

**Files:**
- Modify: `lib/launch.sh`

**Context:** `cmd_launch` currently uses `pwd` as the workspace directory, or resolves a name via `_find_workspace_by_name "$1"`. Phase 3 of setup needs to call `cmd_launch` with an absolute path directly. The change: if `$1` starts with `/`, treat it as a directory path; otherwise treat it as a workspace name (existing behavior).

- [ ] **Step 1: Update `cmd_launch` argument handling in `lib/launch.sh`**

Replace the current argument block (lines ~11-19):
```bash
  # If workspace name is provided as argument, find the directory
  if [[ -n "${1:-}" ]]; then
    local found
    found=$(_find_workspace_by_name "$1")
    if [[ -z "$found" ]]; then
      error "$(t "workspace_not_found"): $1"
      echo "  Registered workspaces: cw list"
      exit 1
    fi
    target_dir="$found"
  fi
```

With:
```bash
  # Argument can be:
  #   /absolute/path  → use directly as workspace directory (called from Phase 3)
  #   name            → look up in registry (existing behavior)
  if [[ -n "${1:-}" ]]; then
    if [[ "${1}" == /* ]]; then
      target_dir="$1"
    else
      local found
      found=$(_find_workspace_by_name "$1")
      if [[ -z "$found" ]]; then
        error "$(t "workspace_not_found"): $1"
        echo "  Registered workspaces: cw list"
        exit 1
      fi
      target_dir="$found"
    fi
  fi
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify syntax**

```bash
bash -n /Users/yurikamo/Developer/github/claude-workspace/lib/launch.sh
```
Expected: no output

- [ ] **Step 3: Smoke-test path resolution (no Claude invocation needed)**

  source lib/i18n.sh
  source lib/registry.sh
  source lib/launch.sh

  # Monkey-patch exec to avoid actually launching claude
  exec() { echo "LAUNCH: $*"; }
  command() { [[ "$1" == "-v" && "$2" == "claude" ]] && return 0; }
  registry_touch() { :; }

  # Test: absolute path argument
  mkdir -p /tmp/cw_test_ws
  echo '"'"'{"name":"Test","description":"","workspace_path":"/tmp/cw_test_ws","created_at":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z","dirs":[]}'"'"' > /tmp/cw_test_ws/.workspace.json
  cd /tmp/cw_test_ws
  cmd_launch /tmp/cw_test_ws 2>/dev/null && echo "PASS: absolute path accepted" || echo "FAIL"
  rm -rf /tmp/cw_test_ws
'
```
Expected: `PASS: absolute path accepted`

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add lib/launch.sh
git commit -m "feat(launch): accept absolute path as optional dir argument"
```

---

## Chunk 2: Core Wizard

### Task 4: Rewrite `lib/setup.sh` — 3-phase wizard

**Files:**
- Modify: `lib/setup.sh`

**Context:** The current `cmd_setup` has 7 sequential steps. Replace with `_setup_phase1`, `_setup_phase2`, `_setup_phase3` internal functions plus the rewritten `cmd_setup`. Module-level variables `_SETUP_NAME`, `_SETUP_DESC`, `_SETUP_DIRS`, `_SETUP_ROLES` carry state between phases (bash 3.2 safe alternative to nameref). The `_generate_workspace_claude_md` and `cmd_add_dir` functions are **unchanged**.

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace `cmd_setup` and add phase functions**

Replace the entire `cmd_setup()` function (lines 6-172 in setup.sh) with the following. Do NOT modify `_generate_workspace_claude_md` or `cmd_add_dir`.

```bash
# Module-level state for wizard phases (bash 3.2 compatible)
_SETUP_NAME=""
_SETUP_DESC=""
_SETUP_DIRS=()
_SETUP_ROLES=()

# ──────────────────────────────
# Phase 1: Workspace name + description
# Sets: _SETUP_NAME, _SETUP_DESC
# Arguments: <default_name>
# ──────────────────────────────
_setup_phase1() {
  local default_name="$1"

  echo ""
  echo "$(bold "$(t "phase1_header")")"
  echo ""

  _SETUP_NAME=$(gum_input "$(t "workspace_name")" "$default_name")
  _SETUP_NAME="${_SETUP_NAME:-$default_name}"

  if [[ -z "$_SETUP_NAME" ]]; then
    error "$(t "workspace_name") $(t "required")"
    exit 1
  fi

  _SETUP_DESC=$(gum_input "$(t "workspace_desc")")
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# Phase 2: Directory collection loop
# Sets: _SETUP_DIRS, _SETUP_ROLES
# ──────────────────────────────
_setup_phase2() {
  _SETUP_DIRS=()
  _SETUP_ROLES=()

  echo ""
  echo "$(bold "$(t "phase2_header")")"
  echo ""
  echo "  $(dim "$(t "setup_path_hint")")"
  echo ""

  while true; do
    # Show added-so-far list
    if [[ ${#_SETUP_DIRS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
      echo "  $(bold "$(t "dirs_added_so_far")")"
      local i
      for i in "${!_SETUP_DIRS[@]}"; do
        local role_label="${_SETUP_ROLES[$i]}"
        local role_display=""
        [[ -n "$role_label" ]] && role_display=" $(dim "(${role_label})")"
        echo "  $(green "✓")  ${_SETUP_DIRS[$i]}${role_display}"
      done
      echo ""
    fi

    local raw_path
    raw_path=$(gum_path_input "$(t "select_dir_prompt")")
    [[ -z "$raw_path" ]] && break

    local expanded_path
    expanded_path=$(expand_path "$raw_path")

    if [[ ! -d "$expanded_path" ]]; then
      gum_error "$(t "dir_not_found"): $expanded_path"
      gum_confirm "$(t "path_add_anyway")" || continue
    fi

    local role
    role=$(gum_input "$(t "dir_add_role")")

    _SETUP_DIRS+=("$expanded_path")
    _SETUP_ROLES+=("${role:-}")
    success "$(t "dir_added"): $expanded_path$([ -n "$role" ] && echo " (${role})")"
    echo ""
  done
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# Phase 3: Summary confirmation + launch choice
# Returns (echoes): LAUNCH | CREATE | CANCEL
# Arguments: <target_dir>
# ──────────────────────────────
_setup_phase3() {
  local target_dir="$1"

  echo ""
  echo "$(bold "$(t "phase3_header")")"
  echo ""

  # Summary box
  echo "  $(bold "$(t "phase3_summary")")"
  echo "  $(dim "────────────────────────────────────────")"
  echo "  $(bold "$(t "info_name")"):     $_SETUP_NAME"
  [[ -n "$_SETUP_DESC" ]] && echo "  $(bold "$(t "info_desc")"):     $_SETUP_DESC"
  echo "  $(bold "$(t "info_path")"):     $target_dir"
  echo "  $(bold "Dirs"):     ${#_SETUP_DIRS[@]}"
  echo "  $(dim "Files"):    .workspace.json, CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/"
  echo "  $(dim "────────────────────────────────────────")"
  echo ""

  local choice
  choice=$(gum_choose \
    "$(t "phase3_opt_launch")" "LAUNCH" \
    "$(t "phase3_opt_create")" "CREATE" \
    "$(t "phase3_opt_cancel")" "CANCEL")

  echo "${choice:-CANCEL}"
}

# ──────────────────────────────
# cmd_setup [prefill_name]
# Main entry point
# ──────────────────────────────
cmd_setup() {
  require_jq

  local prefill_name="${1:-}"
  local target_dir
  target_dir="$(pwd)"

  # Already set up?
  if is_workspace "$target_dir"; then
    warn "$(t "workspace_already_setup")"
    local existing_name
    existing_name=$(ws_get "$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE" '.name')
    echo "  $(t "workspace_name"): $(bold "$existing_name")"
    echo ""
    gum_confirm "$(t "re_setup")" || { info "$(t "cancel")"; exit 0; }
    echo ""
  fi

  echo ""
  echo "$(bold "Claude Workspace Setup")"
  echo "$(dim "$(t "directory"): $target_dir")"

  # ── Phase 1 ──────────────────────────────────
  local default_name
  default_name="$(basename "$target_dir")"
  [[ -n "$prefill_name" ]] && default_name="$prefill_name"
  _setup_phase1 "$default_name"

  # ── Phase 2 ──────────────────────────────────
  _setup_phase2

  # ── Phase 3 ──────────────────────────────────
  local action
  action=$(_setup_phase3 "$target_dir")

  if [[ "$action" == "CANCEL" ]]; then
    info "$(t "cancel")"
    exit 0
  fi

  # ── Execute: write files ──────────────────────
  echo ""
  echo "$(bold "→ $(t "setup_step4")")"

  local ws_file="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE"
  local created_at
  created_at=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

  local dirs_json="[]"
  local i
  for i in "${!_SETUP_DIRS[@]}"; do
    dirs_json=$(echo "$dirs_json" | jq \
      --arg path "${_SETUP_DIRS[$i]}" \
      --arg role "${_SETUP_ROLES[$i]}" \
      '. += [{"path": $path, "role": $role}]')
  done

  jq -n \
    --arg name "$_SETUP_NAME" \
    --arg description "$_SETUP_DESC" \
    --arg created_at "$created_at" \
    --arg workspace_path "$target_dir" \
    --argjson dirs "$dirs_json" \
    '{
      name: $name,
      description: $description,
      workspace_path: $workspace_path,
      created_at: $created_at,
      dirs: $dirs
    }' > "$ws_file"

  success "$(t "created"): $ws_file"

  # ── CLAUDE.md ────────────────────────────────
  echo "$(bold "→ $(t "setup_step5")")"
  local claude_md="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_CLAUDE_MD"
  _generate_workspace_claude_md "$claude_md" "$_SETUP_NAME" "$_SETUP_DESC" "$ws_file"
  success "$(t "created"): $claude_md"

  # ── Skills ───────────────────────────────────
  echo "$(bold "→ $(t "setup_step6")")"
  if [[ -d "$CW_SKILLS_DIR" ]]; then
    local ws_skills_dir="$target_dir/.claude/skills"
    mkdir -p "$ws_skills_dir"
    gum_spin "$(t "setup_step6")" cp -R "$CW_SKILLS_DIR"/. "$ws_skills_dir/"
    success "$(t "setup_skills_installed"): .claude/skills/"
  fi

  # ── Registry ─────────────────────────────────
  echo "$(bold "→ $(t "setup_step7")")"
  registry_add "$_SETUP_NAME" "$target_dir"
  success "$(t "registered"): ~/.claude-workspace/registry.json"

  echo ""
  echo "$(green "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
  echo "$(green "  $(t "setup_complete")")"
  echo "$(green "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
  echo ""

  # ── Launch? ──────────────────────────────────
  if [[ "$action" == "LAUNCH" ]]; then
    cmd_launch "$target_dir"
  else
    echo "  $(t "setup_run")"
    echo "  $(bold "  cw")"
    echo ""
  fi
}
```
